headlines.title: Realty Income Boosts Liquidity: What Does This Mean for Growth?
headlines.summary: O expands liquidity with $5.5B revolving credit facilities and boosts commercial paper capacity to $5.5B from $3B, supporting global growth.

headlines.title: Realty Income's Europe Push: Will It Drive Long-Term Growth Ahead?
headlines.summary: O scales its business in Europe with strong investment activity, competitive yields and a vast market opportunity that could support long-term growth.

headlines.title: Simon Property Group's Quarterly Earnings Preview: What You Need to Know
headlines.summary: Simon Property Group is set to release its second-quarter results soon, with analysts forecasting single-digit growth in earnings.

headlines.title: This Mall REIT Yields Over 4% And Is Up More Than 21% This Year, Wall Street Just Downgraded It Anyway
headlines.summary: Simon Property Group is delivering the kind of performance most REIT investors dream about, so why did Wall Street just pull its Buy rating? A refinancing headwind and a leadership transition raise real questions about whether the premium valuation can hold.

headlines.title: Simon Property Group (SPG) Downgraded at Wolfe Research. Here is Why
headlines.summary: With an annual dividend yield of 3.89%, Simon Property Group, Inc. (NYSE:SPG) is included among the 14 Best Blue Chip Dividend Stocks to Buy According to Hedge Funds. Simon Property Group, Inc. (NYSE:SPG) is a global leader in the ownership of premier shopping, dining, entertainment, and mixed-use destinations and an S&P 100 company.

description: Simon Property Group, Inc. is a self-administered and self-managed real estate investment trust (REIT). Simon Property Group, L.P., or the Operating Partnership, is our majority-owned partnership subsidiary that owns all of our real estate properties and other assets. In this package, the terms Simon, we, our, or the Company refer to Simon Property Group, Inc., the Operating Partnership, and its subsidiaries. We own, develop and manage premier shopping, dining, entertainment and mixed-use destinations, which consist primarily of malls, Premium Outlets, The Mills, and International Properties. At December 31, 2024, we owned or had an interest in 229 properties comprising 183 million square feet in North America, Asia and Europe. We also owned an 88% interest in The Taubman Realty Group, or TRG, which owns 22 regional, super-regional, and outlet malls in the U.S. and Asia. Additionally, at December 31, 2024, we had a 22.4% ownership interest in Klepierre, a publicly traded, Paris-based real estate company, which owns shopping centers in 14 European countries. Simon Property Group, Inc. was incorporated in 1960 and is based in Indianapolis, Indiana.
